title: React form change
prompt: Implement an approved React form change, validate it, review the local diff, and prepare an authorized commit.

title: Rust SQLite query
prompt: Implement a Rust SQLite query correction with migration and query-plan evidence, then review it without pushing.

title: AddrInUse diagnosis
prompt: Diagnose an address-in-use startup failure, apply only the confirmed remediation, and validate the regression seam.

title: Rust memory investigation
prompt: Measure repeated-operation memory growth, confirm the cause, apply the bounded Rust fix, and compare the same workload.

title: Worktree review
prompt: Review a dirty worktree with unrelated edits and return exact ownership labels and safe staging guidance.

title: Commit range review
prompt: Review a fixed commit range across code, tests, security, and docs and return consolidated findings.

title: Frontend architecture audit
prompt: Audit a selected frontend feature for reuse, state ownership, layout, accessibility, and performance.

title: Tokio lifecycle audit
prompt: Audit a known Tokio service for task ownership, cancellation, shutdown, and error propagation.

title: Security audit
prompt: Audit a bounded authentication and session surface and return evidence-grounded risks only.

title: Browser screenshot verification
prompt: Verify a deployed page in the built-in browser at requested viewports and retain screenshot and console evidence.

title: Tauri client verification
prompt: Launch a specified Tauri build, verify the real window and runtime source, and capture semantic-control evidence.

title: Git delivery
prompt: Classify the reviewed paths by intent, create one commit per category, push the current branch, and prove local and remote refs match.

title: Repository ownership map
prompt: Map the real repository roots, ownership boundaries, startup paths, and reusable implementations into a durable navigation artifact.

title: External review package
prompt: Prepare a local external-review package from a fixed commit range and stop before opening a browser or sending it.

title: Source-grounded article rewrite
prompt: Rewrite a supplied technical article in a natural voice while preserving claims, measurements, attribution, and disclosure boundaries.

title: Shared domain decision
prompt: Resolve conflicting shared business language and rules from evidence, loading lifecycle or bounded-context depth only where material complexity requires it, and stop before technical design or repository writes.

title: Monitoring product redesign
prompt: Redesign the monitoring, reporting, and analytics product around Node, Metric, Observation, Alert, Report, and Policy; resolve the domain model, produce an executable specification, implement the approved frontend slices, review Standards and Spec compliance, and deliver only the authorized Git scope.

title: Rust deep-module refactor
prompt: Plan and implement an approved Rust architecture improvement by replacing a shallow forwarding boundary with a deeper module, preserving ownership and error contracts, then review the fixed diff without pushing it.

title: Feature UI with conditional shared system
prompt: Design one evidence-grounded page or flow with applicable visual and interaction states, activate shared-system artifacts only if tokens or component semantics must change, validate the design artifacts, and hand them to frontend implementation without editing source in the design step.

title: Feature product specification
prompt: Inspect existing product facts, clarify only decisions that change behavior, and produce one feature specification that is ready for a named implementation slice without defining technical interfaces or editing source.

title: Bounded API Contract Map
prompt: Verify and record one service operation's repository-native authority, route/DTO/auth owners, client and real consumer, duplicate DTO boundary, available checks, and any existing generated exchange chain as durable navigation; do not introduce OpenAPI.

title: Independent dual review
prompt: Fix one branch basis, build an unbiased package, obtain independent Codex and authorized ChatGPT Project reviews, verify and deduplicate findings locally, fix only confirmed issues, rerun proportionate checks, and finish with one local review; do not repeat ChatGPT unless the basis or a high-risk boundary materially changes.

title: Bounded platform publish
prompt: In the verified account and target, publish one explicitly approved community post exactly once, proving the pre-submit state, direct side effect, and final state while stopping on CAPTCHA, risk control, or ambiguous prior submission.

title: Review-only external challenge
prompt: Obtain one authorized independent ChatGPT review, verify and classify every finding locally, and stop without applying even confirmed findings.

title: Decision research without Git
prompt: Research one pending UI policy decision against primary sources, using the supplied evidence set, date boundary, and exclusions; cite sources and stop before changing any artifact.

title: Authorized post-review fix
prompt: After one independent review, locally verify the findings, implement only the explicitly authorized frontend fix, rerun the original failure path, freeze the new dirty Worktree fingerprint, and finish with Worktree review without committing.

title: Push only
prompt: Push the already reviewed current branch and prove the remote ref, without staging or committing any dirty work.

title: Resolve files only
prompt: Resolve only the named merge conflicts from both sides' primary intent and validate the files; do not stage, continue, commit, or push.

title: Codex-first ChatGPT collaboration
prompt: Interpret my natural request, use local Skills or tools when they can finish it, otherwise use the explicitly authorized ChatGPT web capability that fits the UI, research, architecture, repository, or image outcome; do not require me to write the formal prompt.

title: Evidence-based branch integration
prompt: Integrate the fixed reviewed source branch into the target without pushing: preserve coherent semantic commits when useful, otherwise squash noisy single-outcome history, and account for every omitted commit if partial integration was explicitly requested.
